text: Cystography showed important contrast collection starting at the proximal urethra.
After incision of the peritoneum, the cyst is dissected away from the bladder anteriorly and from the rectum posteriorly.
Cystoscopy during dissection aids in identification of the structure.
After identification of the cyst neck at the level of the urethra, the cyst is translated.
A traction suture is placed at the proximal end of the cyst before complete transection is performed.
After resection, the defect is closed with a barbed wire and the endopelvic fascia is closed.
